title: Firewall
description: Network security device that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing traffic based on rules.
fullExplanation: A firewall enforces a security policy by inspecting network traffic and allowing or blocking it based on rules. Firewalls have evolved from simple packet filters to stateful inspection engines to next-generation firewalls with deep packet inspection. They are the first line of defense in network security.

types.name: Packet Filter
types.description: Inspects IP/port headers only. Stateless — no connection tracking. Fast but limited.

types.name: Stateful Inspection
types.description: Tracks TCP/UDP connection state. Allows return traffic automatically. Most common type.

types.name: Next-Gen Firewall (NGFW)
types.description: Deep packet inspection, application identification, user identity, IPS, SSL inspection.

types.name: Web Application Firewall (WAF)
types.description: Protects HTTP/HTTPS applications from OWASP Top 10 attacks.

keyFacts: Default deny: block everything, allow only what's needed
NGFW can identify apps regardless of port (App-ID)
SSL inspection: decrypt, inspect, re-encrypt (breaks end-to-end encryption)
Zone-based firewall: traffic rules between security zones
Windows Defender Firewall: built-in Windows firewall
